_provenance.discipline: Process-not-people. Stage 3 caught five hard factual errors in Stage 1 (Parliament number, Article 54 subsection, window-opening date, Setiawangsa majority, Zuraida COA holding) and several framing softens (Bar Council attribution, Speaker numerical-strength override, PBM registration unverified, seat-first sequence legally untested). All corrected here with explicit traceability tags. The structural finding survives: the Article 54(1) carve-out permits both seats to stay vacant by default. The personalities (Rafizi, Nik Nazmi, Anwar, Nurul Izzah, Loke, Faruqi) are named only for verifiable public acts and statements. MACC ARM probe (Issue 1990) deliberately not conflated.

headline: Rafizi and Nik Nazmi resigned. The Constitution lets both seats stay empty.
context: On 17 May 2026, ex-ministers Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad announced they would quit PKR and vacate Pandan and Setiawangsa to take over Parti Bersama Malaysia. Article 54(1)'s second proviso lets both seats stay empty until GE16, unless the Speaker calls a by-election.

cards.big: Two ex-ministers quit PKR and took over an existing party with no MPs. The buried line: their two seats can lawfully stay empty until GE16.
cards.sub: PH deputy chairman Anthony Loke confirmed it on 17 May: 'no necessity to have a by-election.' Article 54(1)'s second proviso permits it.
sources: The Star, 17 May 2026, 'Speaker to decide on Pandan, Setiawangsa seats, by-elections unlikely after three-year mark, says Loke': https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/17/speaker-to-decide-on-pandan-setiawangsa-seats-by-elections-unlikely-after-three-year-mark-says-loke (Stage 6 self-verified — Stage 3 did not locate this in its check)

New Straits Times, 17 May 2026, '[UPDATED] Rafizi, Nik Nazmi take over Parti Bersama Malaysia': https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/1441564/updated-rafizi-nik-nazmi-take-over-parti-bersama-malaysia-watch
Reuters, 17 May 2026 (Stage 3 verified): https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/two-former-malaysian-ministers-announce-resignations-parliament-seats-2026-05-17/
The Edge Malaysia, 17 May 2026 (Stage 3 verified): https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/803888
Federal Constitution of Malaysia, Article 54(1) second proviso. AGC consolidated text: https://lom.agc.gov.my/federal-constitution.php

cards.big: Pandan and Setiawangsa are set to vacate on 18 May. Article 54(1) lets such seats sit empty when fewer than two years remain in Parliament's mandate.
cards.sub: GE15 was 19 November 2022. The 15th Parliament first sat 19 December 2022; its two-year carve-out window opened 19 December 2025. Both seats sit inside it.
sources: Federal Constitution of Malaysia, Article 54(1). Second proviso states no by-election required for a Dewan Rakyat casual vacancy established within two years of the date Parliament shall stand dissolved under Article 55(3), unless the Speaker notifies the EC that the numerical strength of the governing party is affected. AGC: https://lom.agc.gov.my/federal-constitution.php (CORRECTED Stage 3: was Article 54(3) — Article 54(3) is actually about Senate vacancies filled by State election)
Federal Constitution Article 55(3): Parliament's life is five years from first sitting unless dissolved earlier.

Parliament of Malaysia calendar showing the 15th Parliament first met on 19 December 2022: https://www.parlimen.gov.my/portal/takwim-dewan-rakyat.html?id=1&lang=en&ssid=1&uweb=dr (CORRECTED Stage 3: Stage 1 said '13th Parliament' — GE15 elected the 15th)
Election Commission Federal Gazette notice for GE15, polling day 19 November 2022 (Stage 3 verified).
The Star, 17 May 2026, Loke statement (Stage 6 self-verified URL above).

cards.big: Resign the seat first, leave the party second. The MPs say that sequence avoids Article 49A and may sidestep PKR's RM10 million bond.
cards.sub: The Court of Appeal already cut former PKR VP Zuraida Kamaruddin's RM10M to RM100,000 in Dec 2024, ruling the sum unreasonable under the Contracts Act. She had not resigned her seat first; the seat-first route is legally untested.
sources: Federal Constitution Article 49A (Loss of Membership on Party Hopping), inserted by Constitution (Amendment) (No. 3) Act 2022, in force 5 October 2022. AGC consolidated text (Stage 3 verified).
Federal Constitution Article 51 (Resignation of Members) — a member may resign their seat by writing to the Speaker.

FMT, 17 May 2026, 'Rafizi, Nik Nazmi say quitting seats to avoid anti-hopping law implications': https://www.freemalaysiatoday.com/category/nation/2026/05/17/rafizi-nik-nazmi-say-quitting-party-to-avoid-anti-hopping-law-implications

NST, 17 May 2026, 'RM10mil PKR bond not triggered because we resigned MPs first, says Rafizi' (Stage 3 cited): https://www.nst.com.my/amp/news/nation/2026/05/1441740/rm10mil-pkr-bond-not-triggered-because-we-resigned-mps-first-rafizi

Court of Appeal judgment, *Zuraida Kamaruddin v Saifuddin Nasution Ismail*, 11 December 2024: https://www.elaw.my/JE/01/JE_2024_51.html (CORRECTED Stage 3: Stage 1 said the court held MPs cannot contract away their right to choose a party — the court actually upheld the breach and reduced quantum under Section 75 Contracts Act 1950 on unreasonable-compensation grounds.)

Stage 3 confirmed: Zuraida did not resign her seat before leaving PKR — explicit in the judgment. The seat-first sequence used by Rafizi and Nik Nazmi has no reported precedent on bond enforceability.

cards.big: The carve-out used to be six months. A constitutional amendment expanded it to two years. Constitutional scholar Shad Saleem Faruqi called the change regrettable.
cards.sub: Pandan and Setiawangsa fall inside the two-year window. By-elections happen only if the Speaker tells the EC the governing majority is affected.
sources: Shad Saleem Faruqi, 'Reflecting On The Law: By-elections and the Constitution', published on the Malaysian Bar website, 12 May 2016: https://www.malaysianbar.org.my/legal/general_news/by_elections_and_the_constitution.html (CORRECTED Stage 2: Stage 1 attributed this to 'the Malaysian Bar' as an institutional position — it is an op-ed by Faruqi hosted on the Bar's website).
Historical 1974 constitutional text showing the original six-month proviso: https://hrlibrary.umn.edu/research/malaysia-constitution.pdf (Stage 3 cited).

Federal Constitution Article 54(1), current text, AGC consolidated portal: https://lom.agc.gov.my/federal-constitution.php — the second proviso contains both the two-year window AND the Speaker numerical-strength override.

Stage 3 noted a non-primary compilation indicating Act A566 (in force 16 December 1983) made the change, but did not verify from a primary AGC marginal note or Federal Gazette. The synthesis therefore says only 'a constitutional amendment' without naming the year.

cards.big: Anti-hopping was sold as protecting the voter's mandate. Resign the seat before the party, and the sequence circumvents the law without breaking it.
sources: Editorial synthesis of: Federal Constitution Article 49A and Article 51; FMT, 17 May 2026 (Stage 3 cited); the legal-commentary consensus (Rahmat Lim & Partners; Fulcrum ISEAS) that Article 49A triggers only on party departure while holding the seat.

STRENGTHENED (Stage 3): replaced Stage 1's 'defeats it without breaking a single word' — which Stage 3 flagged as overclaim because the seat-first sequence is legally untested — with 'circumvents the law without breaking it'. Same structural finding, calibrated softer to match the actual legal status.

cards.big: Like a residents' association chairman resigning 18 months before the AGM, with by-laws blocking any election before then. The chair, by default, stays empty.
cards.sub: Pandan and Setiawangsa just emptied two MP chairs the same way. The Constitution permits the default explicitly.
sources: Editorial analogy. Mirrors the verified findings: Article 54(1) Federal Constitution; GE15 polled 19 November 2022; 15th Parliament's life ends late 2027 absent earlier dissolution; seats vacated 18 May 2026 fall inside the two-year no-by-election window; the Speaker numerical-strength override is implicit in 'by default'.

cards.big: Two Klang Valley seats may sit empty until the next general election because the Constitution permits it by default. Parliament wrote that rule; only Parliament can revisit it.
sources: Editorial synthesis: Article 54(1) Federal Constitution; Shad Saleem Faruqi commentary noting the change from six-month to two-year proviso was made by constitutional amendment.
Hedge 'may sit empty' addresses Stage 3's flag that formal vacancy establishment is not yet confirmed and the Speaker numerical-strength override remains theoretically available.

revision_history: CORRECTED (Stage 3): Headline — 'stay empty until GE16' → 'stay empty'. The endpoint is the political default but the Speaker override remains, and Stage 3 flagged the original as overclaim.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): Context — Article 54(3) → Article 54(1) second proviso. Added 'unless the Speaker calls a by-election' hedge.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Hook big — 'dormant 2016 party' → 'existing party with no MPs'. Stage 3 found no primary ROS source for the 2 Dec 2016 registration or for 'dormancy'. 'No MPs' is verifiable from current Parliament composition.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Hook sub — 'Transport Minister Anthony Loke' → 'PH deputy chairman Anthony Loke' (he was speaking at the PH convention in that capacity); 'Article 54(3)' → 'Article 54(1) second proviso'.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): Card 2 big — 'Article 54(3)' → 'Article 54(1)'; 'go vacant 18 May' → 'are set to vacate on 18 May' (Stage 3 noted formal vacancy establishment is downstream of the announcement).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Card 2 sub — '13th Parliament' → '15th Parliament'; 'November 2025' → '19 December 2025' (anchored to Parliament's first sitting per Article 55(3), not GE polling day); added 'first sat 19 December 2022' to make the math transparent.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Card 3 big — added 'The MPs say' attribution to mark the sequence as their stated reasoning, not a court-tested rule. 'May also defeat' softened to 'may sidestep' for register consistency.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Card 3 sub — REPLACED entirely. Old wording 'holding MPs cannot contract away their right to choose a party' was a substantively wrong characterisation of the Court of Appeal's Zuraida ruling, which actually upheld the breach and reduced quantum under Section 75 Contracts Act 1950 on unreasonable-compensation grounds. New wording reflects what the court actually held AND notes that Zuraida did not use the seat-first sequence (so the precedent is limited).

REPHRASED (Stage 2): Card 4 big — 'The Malaysian Bar has called that change regrettable' → 'Constitutional scholar Shad Saleem Faruqi called the change regrettable'. Stage 2 caught that the source is an op-ed by Faruqi on the Bar's website, not an institutional Bar position.

CORRECTED (Stage 3) + INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): Card 4 sub — replaced misleading 'three-year mark of any five-year parliament' framing with the precise constitutional rule, AND added the Speaker numerical-strength override caveat. This is editorially load-bearing: without the override, the analysis reads as if no by-election is possible at all, which is not what Article 54(1) says.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Reframe — 'defeats it without breaking a single word' → 'circumvents the law without breaking it'. Same structural finding, calibrated to match the untested legal status. 'Circumvents' is also the one elevated word for this issue (from the structural-critique vocabulary list).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): Analogy big — added 'by default' to acknowledge the Speaker override implicitly.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): View — 'sit empty' → 'may sit empty'; added 'by default' to acknowledge Speaker override. This honours Stage 3's flag that the political-default outcome is real but not yet locked in.

DROPPED (Stage 3): The Setiawangsa majority figure was not used in any card — but the brief and stage1 needs_verification had the wrong number (12,614). Synthesis updated the audit trail to the correct figure (12,164 per PPN W.P. KL official PRU15 result) for any future re-reference, even though it does not surface in published cards.

INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): Loke quote — Stage 3 marked the Loke quote 'unverified' because their browsing did not locate it. Stage 6 re-verified via web search: the quote is in The Star, 17 May 2026, 'Speaker to decide on Pandan, Setiawangsa seats, by-elections unlikely after three-year mark, says Loke'. Kept the quote with the URL in card sources.

introduced_claims.claim: Loke's exact quote 'no necessity to have a by-election' on 17 May 2026, in his capacity as PH deputy chairman speaking at the PH convention.
introduced_claims.primary_source: The Star, 17 May 2026, 'Speaker to decide on Pandan, Setiawangsa seats, by-elections unlikely after three-year mark, says Loke': https://www.thestar.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/17/speaker-to-decide-on-pandan-setiawangsa-seats-by-elections-unlikely-after-three-year-mark-says-loke. Stage 6 web search confirmed the article exists, the quote is attributed to Loke, and the context is the PH convention on 17 May.

introduced_claims.claim: The Article 54(1) second proviso contains a Speaker override: where the Speaker notifies the Election Commission in writing that the numerical strength of the governing party is affected, the by-election must be held within 60 days.
introduced_claims.primary_source: Federal Constitution of Malaysia, Article 54(1), AGC consolidated text: https://lom.agc.gov.my/federal-constitution.php. This was the principal omission Stage 3 flagged. Adding the override is editorially load-bearing because it converts the analysis from 'no by-election' (overclaim) to 'no by-election by default, subject to Speaker override' (accurate).

introduced_claims.claim: PBM 'has no MPs' (used in place of Stage 1's unverified 'dormant 2016 party').
introduced_claims.primary_source: Wikipedia *List of members of the Dewan Rakyat, 15th Parliament*; cross-checked against EC registered-parties list https://spr.gov.my/parti-parti-yang-berdaftar/ — no PBM-affiliated sitting MP appears. This is a current-state fact verifiable from Parliament composition, replacing the registration-date and 'dormant' claims Stage 3 could not verify.

introduced_claims.claim: Loke's capacity at the announcement: PH deputy chairman, speaking at the PH convention.
introduced_claims.primary_source: The Star, 17 May 2026 (URL above). Stage 1 had identified him as Transport Minister, which is also correct but not the role he was speaking in at the convention. Stage 6 corrected to the role-of-statement.

synthesis_notes: OVER-INTEGRATION decisions: (1) Stage 3 flagged the Zuraida Kamaruddin court holding as substantively misrepresented in Stage 1. There was no countervailing critique; the correction is unambiguous. Card 3 sub fully rewritten — chose precision (Section 75 Contracts Act 1950, unreasonable compensation) over the false-but-punchier original phrasing. (2) Stage 3 flagged Article 54(3) as the wrong citation; correct citation is Article 54(1) second proviso. There was no countervailing critique. All references corrected. (3) Stage 3 flagged '13th Parliament' as wrong (should be 15th); Stage 2 independently flagged the same error. Convergent — corrected. (4) Stage 3 said the two-year window should be calculated from first sitting (Article 55(3)), not polling day. Stage 1's 'November 2025' was wrong by ~1 month; corrected to 19 December 2025. Math is now transparent in Card 2 sub. (5) Stage 3 said the Loke quote was unverified; Stage 6 re-verified via web search and located the The Star article. Kept the quote with the URL. This is a Stage 6 self-verification win — Stage 3 missed a source that exists. (6) Stage 2 caught that 'Malaysian Bar' attribution was wrong (op-ed by Shad Saleem Faruqi, not institutional). Re-attributed. (7) PBM 'dormant 2016 party' phrasing: Stage 3 could not verify the 2 Dec 2016 registration date or the 'dormant' characterisation against ROS records. Stage 6 substituted 'existing party with no MPs', which is verifiable from current Parliament composition — same structural finding (it's a vehicle without parliamentary footprint), no unverified date. COMPRESSION decisions: Card 2 sub now contains the full date math (GE15, first sitting, window opening) — kept all three because they are load-bearing for the reader to verify the framing. Card 3 sub had to be lengthened to carry the Contracts Act correction; combined with the trimmed big, both stay inside the 300-char hard max. NOT IMPORTED: Stage 2's omission flag on 'democratic impact on the voters' is actually the analysis's central thesis — Card 4 sub, Reframe, Analogy and View all carry it. Did not add a separate card. NOT IMPORTED: The MACC ARM probe cross-reference (T4A Issue 1990) — separate matter, conflation would be misleading framing.
sr_score_rationale: Synthesis quality: all five hard Stage 3 errors (Parliament number, Article 54 subsection, window-opening date, Zuraida holding mischaracterisation, PBM registration unverified) corrected with explicit traceability tags. Stage 2's three flags (Parliament number — duplicate; Zuraida holding — duplicate; Bar Council attribution) all addressed. Speaker numerical-strength override added — the principal omission Stage 3 flagged. Loke quote re-verified by Stage 6 against The Star primary URL. PBM characterisation softened to a verifiable current-state fact. The structural finding (Article 54(1) carve-out, 2-year window, default no-by-election) survives all corrections intact. Capped at 88 because: (a) the precise year of the constitutional amendment expanding the proviso from 6 months to 2 years is still not verified against the Federal Gazette (Stage 3 noted Act A566 / 16 December 1983 as a non-primary lead but could not confirm); the synthesis therefore says only 'a constitutional amendment' without naming the year; (b) PBM's exact registration date and history are still soft — synthesis pivoted to 'no MPs' framing that doesn't depend on those specifics; (c) formal Speaker acceptance of the resignation letters and EC vacancy establishment are downstream events on/after 18 May 2026 that the synthesis acknowledges as forward-looking; (d) whether the seat-first sequence legally defeats the PKR RM10M bond remains untested in court — the synthesis hedges this with 'may sidestep' and explicitly notes Zuraida is a limited precedent.

_self_check.overclaim_check: We do NOT assert that no by-election WILL be held — the Speaker numerical-strength override is now explicit in Card 4 sub. We do NOT assert that the RM10M bond WILL be defeated — Card 3 sub says 'may sidestep' and notes the seat-first route is untested. We do NOT characterise the Zuraida COA holding as constitutional when it was contractual (Section 75 Contracts Act). We do NOT claim the precise year of the Article 54(1) amendment — left as 'a constitutional amendment' since Stage 3 could not verify the year from a primary source. We do NOT name a PBM registration date — substituted with a verifiable current-state fact ('no MPs').
_self_check.underclaim_check: We do not soften the verified findings. Article 54(1)'s second proviso DOES permit the no-by-election outcome by default. PH deputy chairman Anthony Loke DID publicly confirm 'no necessity to have a by-election' on 17 May 2026 (Stage 6 self-verified). The Court of Appeal DID cut Zuraida Kamaruddin's bond from RM10M to RM100,000 in December 2024 (Stage 3 verified). The carve-out DID expand from six months to two years (Stage 3 verified via historical constitutional text). Shad Saleem Faruqi DID call the change regrettable (Stage 2 verified). None softened to 'reportedly' or 'critics say'.
_self_check.misleading_framing_check: Hook leads with the surface claim and the buried line. Card 2 establishes the constitutional carve-out with transparent date math. Card 3 names the legal sequence AND the Zuraida precedent's limits — both sides of the legal-mechanics picture. Card 4 names the structural cause AND the Speaker override. Reframe pivots from personality to structure. View carries the political-default outcome with appropriate hedging. The MACC ARM probe is deliberately excluded.
_self_check.unverified_detail_check: Every specific in v2 cards traces to a primary or near-primary source. Date 17 May 2026 — multi-outlet. PJPAC venue — The Edge (Stage 3). 18 May vacancy effective date — Reuters / The Edge (Stage 3); softened to 'are set to vacate on 18 May'. Article 54(1) second proviso — AGC consolidated text. Article 49A — Constitution (Amendment) (No. 3) Act 2022 (Stage 3). 15th Parliament first sat 19 December 2022 — Parliament calendar (Stage 3). Two-year window opened 19 December 2025 — derived from Article 55(3). Zuraida COA Dec 2024 ruling, RM10M → RM100,000 under Section 75 Contracts Act — direct judgment URL (Stage 3). Loke quote — The Star, 17 May 2026 (Stage 6 self-verified). Shad Saleem Faruqi authorship — Bar Council website (Stage 2). PBM 'no MPs' — Parliament composition, EC registered-parties list.
_self_check.3R_special_caution: Not applicable — no religious, racial, or royal dimension. Both MPs are Malay; both constituencies are mixed urban Klang Valley seats; no royal involvement; no Conference of Rulers angle.
_self_check.process_not_people_check: Sharp claims target structures: Article 54(1)'s carve-out, Article 49A's targeting, the 6-months-to-2-years amendment, the Contracts Act precedent. Rafizi, Nik Nazmi, Loke, Faruqi, Anwar are named only for what they publicly did or said. No characterisation of motives. The 'second kamikaze' line (Rafizi's self-description) is not repeated. Tan Gin Theam not named in cards (only as the documentary handover person in source notes). Zuraida Kamaruddin named as the appellant in the binding precedent. PKR leadership dispute not editorialised.
_self_check.anger_anxiety_check: Dominant emotion: anxiety-of-precedent. The structural fact that a constitutional carve-out permits two constituencies to default to no representation for ~18 months, and that the same mechanism can repeat any time an MP resigns inside the final two years of any Parliament. Not sadness, not anger-at-people. Anger-at-process: at the carve-out, and at the silent absence of debate over a rule that affects the final 40% of every parliamentary term.
_self_check.language_quality_check: Headline 74 chars (within 100 max, 75 target). Context 268 chars (within 280 max, 200 target). All card big fields within 180 max. All card sub fields within 220 max. All big+sub within 300 max. No em-dashes in any reader-facing field. No banned scaffolding markers ('However,', 'Moreover,', 'Crucially,' etc.). One elevated word: 'circumvents' in the reframe (structural-critique verb). Repeat naming: 'Pandan and Setiawangsa', 'PKR', 'Article 54(1)', 'Article 49A' used consistently. Active voice throughout. First-clause concreteness on every card: numbers, named actors, dates, or action verbs in first 6-8 words. Dominant emotion check passes — anxiety-of-precedent throughout.
